NKJV.1: Whoever believes that Jesus is the Christ is born of God, and everyone who loves Him who begot also loves him who is begotten of Him.
NKJV.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and keep His commandments.
NKJV.3: For this is the love of God, that we keep His commandments. And His commandments are not burdensome.
NKJV.4: For whatever is born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world—our faith.
NKJV.5: Who is he who overcomes the world, but he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
NKJV.6: This is He who came by water and blood—Jesus Christ; not only by water, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit who bears witness, because the Spirit is truth.
NKJV.7: For there are three that bear witness in heaven: the Father, the Word, and the Holy Spirit; and these three are one.
NKJV.8: And there are three that bear witness on earth: the Spirit, the water, and the blood; and these three agree as one.
NKJV.9: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; for this is the witness of God which He has testified of His Son.
NKJV.10: He who believes in the Son of God has the witness in himself; he who does not believe God has made Him a liar, because he has not believed the testimony that God has given of His Son.
NKJV.11: And this is the testimony: that God has given us eternal life, and this life is in His Son.
NKJV.12: He who has the Son has life; he who does not have the Son of God does not have life.
NKJV.13: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may continue to believe in the name of the Son of God.
NKJV.14: Now this is the confidence that we have in Him, that if we ask anything according to His will, He hears us.
NKJV.15: And if we know that He hears us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the petitions that we have asked of Him.
NKJV.16: If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin which does not lead to death, he will ask, and He will give him life for those who commit sin not leading to death. There is sin leading to death. I do not say that he should pray about that.
NKJV.17: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is sin not leading to death.
NKJV.18: We know that whoever is born of God does not sin; but he who has been born of God keeps himself, and the wicked one does not touch him.
NKJV.19: We know that we are of God, and the whole world lies under the sway of the wicked one.
NKJV.20: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us an understanding, that we may know Him who is true; and we are in Him who is true, in His Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
NKJV.21: Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen.

ESV.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, and everyone who loves the Father loves whoever has been born of him.
ESV.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and obey his commandments.
ESV.3: For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome.
ESV.4: For everyone who has been born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world — our faith.
ESV.5: Who is it that overcomes the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
ESV.6: This is he who came by water and blood — Jesus Christ; not by the water only but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
ESV.7: For there are three that testify:
ESV.8: the Spirit and the water and the blood; and these three agree.
ESV.9: If we receive the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater, for this is the testimony of God that he has borne concerning his Son.
ESV.10: Whoever believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. Whoever does not believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has borne concerning his Son.
ESV.11: And this is the testimony, that God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
ESV.12: Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
ESV.13: I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life.
ESV.14: And this is the confidence that we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will he hears us.
ESV.15: And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests that we have asked of him.
ESV.16: If anyone sees his brother committing a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God  will give him life — to those who commit sins that do not lead to death. There is sin that leads to death; I do not say that one should pray for that.
ESV.17: All wrongdoing is sin, but there is sin that does not lead to death.
ESV.18: We know that everyone who has been born of God does not keep on sinning, but he who was born of God protects him, and the evil one does not touch him.
ESV.19: We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
ESV.20: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
ESV.21: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

NASB.1: Whoever believes that Jesus is the Christ is born of God, and whoever loves the Father loves the [child] born of Him.
NASB.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and observe His commandments.
NASB.3: For this is the love of God, that we keep His commandments; and His commandments are not burdensome.
NASB.4: For whatever is born of God overcomes the world; and this is the victory that has overcome the world-- our faith.
NASB.5: Who is the one who overcomes the world, but he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
NASB.6: This is the One who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ; not with the water only, but with the water and with the blood. It is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
NASB.7: For there are three that testify:
NASB.8: the Spirit and the water and the blood; and the three are in agreement.
NASB.9: If we receive the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater; for the testimony of God is this, that He has testified concerning His Son.
NASB.10: The one who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself; the one who does not believe God has made Him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has given concerning His Son.
NASB.11: And the testimony is this, that God has given us eternal life, and this life is in His Son.
NASB.12: He who has the Son has the life; he who does not have the Son of God does not have the life.
NASB.13: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, so that you may know that you have eternal life.
NASB.14: This is the confidence which we have before Him, that, if we ask anything according to His will, He hears us.
NASB.15: And if we know that He hears us [in] whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests which we have asked from Him.
NASB.16: If anyone sees his brother committing a sin not [leading] to death, he shall ask and [God] will for him give life to those who commit sin not [leading] to death. There is a sin [leading] to death; I do not say that he should make request for this.
NASB.17: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is a sin not [leading] to death.
NASB.18: We know that no one who is born of God sins; but He who was born of God keeps him, and the evil one does not touch him.
NASB.19: We know that we are of God, and that the whole world lies in [the power of] the evil one.
NASB.20: And we know that the Son of God has come, and has given us understanding so that we may know Him who is true; and we are in Him who is true, in His Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
NASB.21: Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

NLT.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has become a child of God. And everyone who loves the Father loves his children, too.
NLT.2: We know we love God’s children if we love God and obey his commandments.
NLT.3: Loving God means keeping his commandments, and his commandments are not burdensome.
NLT.4: For every child of God defeats this evil world, and we achieve this victory through our faith.
NLT.5: And who can win this battle against the world? Only those who believe that Jesus is the Son of God.
NLT.6: And Jesus Christ was revealed as God’s Son by his baptism in water and by shedding his blood on the cross — not by water only, but by water and blood. And the Spirit, who is truth, confirms it with his testimony.
NLT.7: So we have these three witnesses —
NLT.8: the Spirit, the water, and the blood — and all three agree.
NLT.9: Since we believe human testimony, surely we can believe the greater testimony that comes from God. And God has testified about his Son.
NLT.10: All who believe in the Son of God know in their hearts that this testimony is true. Those who don’t believe this are actually calling God a liar because they don’t believe what God has testified about his Son.
NLT.11: And this is what God has testified: He has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
NLT.12: Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have God’s Son does not have life.
NLT.13: I have written this to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, so that you may know you have eternal life.
NLT.14: And we are confident that he hears us whenever we ask for anything that pleases him.
NLT.15: And since we know he hears us when we make our requests, we also know that he will give us what we ask for.
NLT.16: If you see a fellow believer sinning in a way that does not lead to death, you should pray, and God will give that person life. But there is a sin that leads to death, and I am not saying you should pray for those who commit it.
NLT.17: All wicked actions are sin, but not every sin leads to death.
NLT.18: We know that God’s children do not make a practice of sinning, for God’s Son holds them securely, and the evil one cannot touch them.
NLT.19: We know that we are children of God and that the world around us is under the control of the evil one.
NLT.20: And we know that the Son of God has come, and he has given us understanding so that we can know the true God. And now we live in fellowship with the true God because we live in fellowship with his Son, Jesus Christ. He is the only true God, and he is eternal life.
NLT.21: Dear children, keep away from anything that might take God’s place in your hearts.

WEB.1: Whoever believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God. Whoever loves the Father also loves the child who is born of him.
WEB.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and keep his commandments.
WEB.3: For this is loving God, that we keep his commandments. His commandments are not grievous.
WEB.4: For whatever is born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world: your faith.
WEB.5: Who is he who overcomes the world, but he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
WEB.6: This is he who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ; not with the water only, but with the water and the blood. It is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
WEB.7: For there are three who testify :
WEB.8: the Spirit, the water, and the blood; and the three agree as one.
WEB.9: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; for this is God’s testimony which he has testified concerning his Son.
WEB.10: He who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. He who doesn’t believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.
WEB.11: The testimony is this, that God gave to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
WEB.12: He who has the Son has the life. He who doesn’t have God’s Son doesn’t have the life.
WEB.13: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may continue to believe in the name of the Son of God.
WEB.14: This is the boldness which we have toward him, that, if we ask anything according to his will, he listens to us.
WEB.15: And if we know that he listens to us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the petitions which we have asked of him.
WEB.16: If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life for those who sin not leading to death. There is a sin leading to death. I don’t say that he should make a request concerning this.
WEB.17: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is a sin not leading to death.
WEB.18: We know that whoever is born of God doesn’t sin, but he who was born of God keeps himself, and the evil one doesn’t touch him.
WEB.19: We know that we are of God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
WEB.20: We know that the Son of God has come, and has given us an understanding, that we know him who is true, and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life.
WEB.21: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

CSB17.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and everyone who loves the Father <sup>[1]</sup> also loves the one born of him.
CSB17.2: This is how we know that we love God’s children: when we love God and obey <sup>[2]</sup> his commands.
CSB17.3: For this is what love for God is: to keep his commands. And his commands are not a burden, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: because everyone who has been born of God conquers the world. This is the victory that has conquered the world: our faith.
CSB17.5: Who is the one who conquers the world <sup>ⓓ</sup> but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God? <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.6: Jesus Christ — he is the one who came by water and blood, not by water only, but by water <sup>ⓕ</sup> and by blood. <sup>ⓖ</sup> And the Spirit is the one who testifies, <sup>ⓗ</sup> because the Spirit is the truth.
CSB17.7: For there are three that testify: <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.8: the Spirit, <sup>ⓙ</sup> the water, and the blood <sup>ⓚ</sup>  — and these three are in agreement.
CSB17.9: If we accept human testimony, God’s testimony <sup>ⓛ</sup> is greater, because it is God’s testimony that he has given about his Son.
CSB17.10: The one who believes in the Son of God has this testimony within himself. The one who does not believe God has made him a liar, <sup>ⓜ</sup> because he has not believed in the testimony God has given about his Son.
CSB17.11: And this is the testimony: God has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
CSB17.12: The one who has the Son has life. <sup>ⓝ</sup> The one who does not have the Son of God does not have life.
CSB17.13: I have written these things to you who believe in the name <sup>ⓐ</sup> of the Son of God so that you may know that you have eternal life.
CSB17.14: This is the confidence we have before him: If we ask <sup>ⓑ</sup> anything according to his will, he hears us. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.15: And if we know that he hears whatever we ask, <sup>ⓓ</sup> we know that we have what we have asked of him. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.16: If anyone sees a fellow believer <sup>[4]</sup> committing a sin that doesn’t lead to death, he should ask, and God will give life to him — to those who commit sin that doesn’t lead to death. There is sin <sup>[5]</sup> that leads to death. I am not saying he should pray about that.
CSB17.17: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is sin that doesn’t lead to death.
CSB17.18: We know that everyone who has been born of God does not sin, but the one who is born of God keeps him, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> and the evil one does not touch him.
CSB17.19: We know that we are of God, and the whole world is under the sway of the evil one.
CSB17.20: And we know that the Son of God has come <sup>ⓖ</sup> and has given us understanding so that we may know the true one. <sup>[7]</sup> We are in the true one — that is, in his Son Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓗ</sup> He is the true God and eternal life.
CSB17.21: Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

AMP.1: Everyone who believes [with a deep, abiding trust in the fact] that Jesus is the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) is born of God [that is, reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose], and everyone who loves the Father also loves the child born of Him.
AMP.2: By this we know [without any doubt] that we love the children of God: [expressing that love] when we love God and obey His commandments.
AMP.3: For the [true] love of God is this: that we habitually keep His commandments and remain focused on His precepts. And His commandments and His precepts are not difficult [to obey].
AMP.4: For everyone born of God is victorious and overcomes the world; and this is the victory that has conquered and overcome the world—our [continuing, persistent] faith [in Jesus the Son of God].
AMP.5: Who is the one who is victorious and overcomes the world? It is the one who believes and recognizes the fact that Jesus is the Son of God.
AMP.6: This is He who came through water and blood [His baptism and death], Jesus Christ—not by the water only, but by the water and the blood. It is the [Holy] Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth. [He is the essence and origin of truth itself.]
AMP.8: the Spirit and the water and the blood; and these three are in agreement [their testimony is perfectly consistent].
AMP.9: If we accept [as we do] the testimony of men [that is, if we are willing to take the sworn statements of fallible humans as evidence], the testimony of God is greater [far more authoritative]; for this is the testimony of God, that He has testified regarding His Son.
AMP.10: The one who believes in the Son of God [who adheres to, trusts in, and relies confidently on Him as Savior] has the testimony within himself [because he can speak authoritatively about Christ from his own personal experience]. The one who does not believe God [in this way] has made Him [out to be] a liar, because he has not believed in the evidence that God has given regarding His Son.
AMP.11: And the testimony is this: God has given us eternal life [we already possess it], and this life is in His Son [resulting in our spiritual completeness, and eternal companionship with Him].
AMP.12: He who has the Son [by accepting Him as Lord and Savior] has the life [that is eternal]; he who does not have the Son of God [by personal faith] does not have the life.
AMP.13: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God [which represents all that Jesus Christ is and does], so that you will know [with settled and absolute knowledge] that you [already] have eternal life.
AMP.14: This is the [remarkable degree of] confidence which we [as believers are entitled to] have before Him: that if we ask anything according to His will, [that is, consistent with His plan and purpose] He hears us.
AMP.15: And if we know [for a fact, as indeed we do] that He hears and listens to us in whatever we ask, we [also] know [with settled and absolute knowledge] that we have [granted to us] the requests which we have asked from Him.
AMP.16: If anyone sees his brother committing a sin that does not lead to death, he will pray and ask [on the believer's behalf] and God will for him give life to those whose sin is not leading to death. There is a sin that leads to death; I do not say that one should pray for this [kind of sin].
AMP.17: All wrongdoing is sin, and there is sin that does not lead to death [one can repent of it and be forgiven].
AMP.18: We know [with confidence] that anyone born of God does not habitually sin; but He (Jesus) who was born of God [carefully] keeps and protects him, and the evil one does not touch him.
AMP.19: We know [for a fact] that we are of God, and the whole world [around us] lies in the power of the evil one [opposing God and His precepts].
AMP.20: And we [have seen and] know [by personal experience] that the Son of God has [actually] come [to this world], and has given us understanding and insight so that we may [progressively and personally] know Him who is true; and we are in Him who is true—in His Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
AMP.21: Little children (believers, dear ones), guard yourselves from idols—[false teachings, moral compromises, and anything that would take God's place in your heart].

MSG.1: Every person who believes that Jesus is, in fact, the Messiah, is God-begotten. If we love the One who conceives the child, we'll surely love the child who was conceived.
MSG.2: The reality test on whether or not we love God's children is this: Do we love God? Do we keep his commands?
MSG.3: The proof that we love God comes when we keep his commandments and they are not at all troublesome.
MSG.4: Every God-begotten person conquers the world's ways. The conquering power that brings the world to its knees is our faith.
MSG.5: The person who wins out over the world's ways is simply the one who believes Jesus is the Son of God.
MSG.6: Jesus—the Divine Christ! He experienced a life-giving birth and a death-killing death. Not only birth from the womb, but baptismal birth of his ministry and sacrificial death. And all the while the Spirit is confirming the truth, the reality of God's presence at Jesus' baptism and crucifixion, bringing those occasions alive for us.
MSG.8: the Spirit, the Baptism, the Crucifixion. And the three in perfect agreement.
MSG.9: If we take human testimony at face value, how much more should we be reassured when God gives testimony as he does here, testifying concerning his Son.
MSG.10: Whoever believes in the Son of God inwardly confirms God's testimony. Whoever refuses to believe in effect calls God a liar, refusing to believe God's own testimony regarding his Son.
MSG.11: This is the testimony in essence: God gave us eternal life; the life is in his Son.
MSG.12: So, whoever has the Son, has life; whoever rejects the Son, rejects life.
MSG.13: My purpose in writing is simply this: that you who believe in God's Son will know beyond the shadow of a doubt that you have eternal life, the reality and not the illusion.
MSG.14: And how bold and free we then become in his presence, freely asking according to his will, sure that he's listening.
MSG.15: And if we're confident that he's listening, we know that what we've asked for is as good as ours.
MSG.16: For instance, if we see a Christian believer sinning (clearly I'm not talking about those who make a practice of sin in a way that is "fatal," leading to eternal death), we ask for God's help and he gladly gives it, gives life to the sinner whose sin is not fatal. There is such a thing as a fatal sin, and I'm not urging you to pray about that.
MSG.17: Everything we do wrong is sin, but not all sin is fatal.
MSG.18: We know that none of the God-begotten makes a practice of sin—fatal sin. The God-begotten are also the God-protected. The Evil One can't lay a hand on them.
MSG.19: We know that we are held firm by God; it's only the people of the world who continue in the grip of the Evil One.
MSG.20: And we know that the Son of God came so we could recognize and understand the truth of God—what a gift!—and we are living in the Truth itself, in God's Son, Jesus Christ. This Jesus is both True God and Real Life.
MSG.21: Dear children, be on guard against all clever facsimiles.

NIV.1:
Faith in the Son of God
Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ is born of God, and everyone who loves the father loves his child as well.
NIV.2: This is how we know that we love the children of God: by loving God and carrying out his commands.
NIV.3: This is love for God: to obey his commands. And his commands are not burdensome,
NIV.4: for everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world, even our faith.
NIV.5: Who is it that overcomes the world? Only he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God.
NIV.6: This is the one who came by water and blood—Jesus Christ. He did not come by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
NIV.7: For there are three that testify:
NIV.8: the Spirit, the water and the blood; and the three are in agreement.
NIV.9: We accept man’s testimony, but God’s testimony is greater because it is the testimony of God, which he has given about his Son.
NIV.10: Anyone who believes in the Son of God has this testimony in his heart. Anyone who does not believe God has made him out to be a liar, because he has not believed the testimony God has given about his Son.
NIV.11: And this is the testimony: God has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
NIV.12: He who has the Son has life; he who does not have the Son of God does not have life.
NIV.13:
Concluding Remarks
I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God so that you may know that you have eternal life.
NIV.14: This is the confidence we have in approaching God: that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
NIV.15: And if we know that he hears us—whatever we ask—we know that we have what we asked of him.
NIV.16: If anyone sees his brother commit a sin that does not lead to death, he should pray and God will give him life. I refer to those whose sin does not lead to death. There is a sin that leads to death. I am not saying that he should pray about that.
NIV.17: All wrongdoing is sin, and there is sin that does not lead to death.
NIV.18: We know that anyone born of God does not continue to sin; the one who was born of God keeps him safe, and the evil one cannot harm him.
NIV.19: We know that we are children of God, and that the whole world is under the control of the evil one.
NIV.20: We know also that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know him who is true. And we are in him who is true—even in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
NIV.21: Dear children, keep yourselves from idols.

NRSVCE.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, and everyone who loves the parent loves the child.
NRSVCE.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and obey his commandments.
NRSVCE.3: For the love of God is this, that we obey his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome,
NRSVCE.4: for whatever is born of God conquers the world. And this is the victory that conquers the world, our faith.
NRSVCE.5: Who is it that conquers the world but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
NRSVCE.6: This is the one who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ, not with the water only but with the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one that testifies, for the Spirit is the truth.
NRSVCE.8: the Spirit and the water and the blood, and these three agree.
NRSVCE.9: If we receive human testimony, the testimony of God is greater; for this is the testimony of God that he has testified to his Son.
NRSVCE.10: Those who believe in the Son of God have the testimony in their hearts. Those who do not believe in God have made him a liar by not believing in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.
NRSVCE.11: And this is the testimony: God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
NRSVCE.12: Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
NRSVCE.13: I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, so that you may know that you have eternal life.
NRSVCE.14: And this is the boldness we have in him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
NRSVCE.15: And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask, we know that we have obtained the requests made of him.
NRSVCE.16: If you see your brother or sister committing what is not a mortal sin, you will ask, and God will give life to such a one — to those whose sin is not mortal. There is sin that is mortal; I do not say that you should pray about that.
NRSVCE.17: All wrongdoing is sin, but there is sin that is not mortal.
NRSVCE.18: We know that those who are born of God do not sin, but the one who was born of God protects them, and the evil one does not touch them.
NRSVCE.19: We know that we are God’s children, and that the whole world lies under the power of the evil one.
NRSVCE.20: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
NRSVCE.21: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

NET.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been fathered by God, and everyone who loves the father loves the child fathered by him.
NET.2: By this we know that we love the children of God: whenever we love God and obey his commandments.
NET.3: For this is the love of God: that we keep his commandments. And his commandments do not weigh us down,
NET.4: because everyone who has been fathered by God conquers the world. This is the conquering power that has conquered the world: our faith.
NET.5: Now who is the person who has conquered the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
NET.6: Jesus Christ is the one who came by water and blood — not by the water only, but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
NET.7: For there are three that testify,
NET.8: the Spirit and the water and the blood, and these three are in agreement.
NET.9: If we accept the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater, because this is the testimony of God that he has testified concerning his Son.
NET.10: (The one who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself; the one who does not believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has testified concerning his Son.)
NET.11: And this is the testimony: God has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
NET.12: The one who has the Son has this eternal life; the one who does not have the Son of God does not have this eternal life.
NET.13: I have written these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God so that you may know that you have eternal life.
NET.14: And this is the confidence that we have before him: that whenever we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
NET.15: And if we know that he hears us in regard to whatever we ask, then we know that we have the requests that we have asked from him.
NET.16: If anyone sees his fellow Christian committing a sin not resulting in death, he should ask, and God will grant life to the person who commits a sin not resulting in death. There is a sin resulting in death. I do not say that he should ask about that.
NET.17: All unrighteousness is sin, but there is sin not resulting in death.
NET.18: We know that everyone fathered by God does not sin, but God protects the one he has fathered, and the evil one cannot touch him.
NET.19: We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
NET.20: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us insight to know him who is true, and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. This one is the true God and eternal life.
NET.21: Little children, guard yourselves from idols.
